findings.message: api_secret_key is typed as plain str rather than pydantic.SecretStr. This means the secret value will appear in plaintext in Settings.__repr__, model_dump(), and any log line that serialises the settings object, which can leak the key to logs or error traces.
findings.suggestion: Change the field type to pydantic.SecretStr: `from pydantic import SecretStr` and declare `api_secret_key: SecretStr`. Access the raw value only where needed via `settings.api_secret_key.get_secret_value()`.

This means the secret value will appear in plaintext in Settings.__repr__, model_dump(), and any log line that serialises the settings object, which can leak the key to logs or error traces.","suggestion":"Change the field type to pydantic.SecretStr: `from pydantic import SecretStr` and declare `api_secret_key: SecretStr`. Access the raw value only where needed via `settings.api_secret_key.get_secret_value()`."}]
